## Workflow Compliance

- [ ] CHK001 Each required spec-kit stage is owned by a dedicated agent
- [ ] CHK002 Review-agent checkpoints are defined for every critical artifact
- [ ] CHK003 Inter-agent communication contracts and handoff metadata are documented

## Architecture Compliance

- [ ] CHK004 Backend implementation follows the Python-first rule or records an approved exception
- [ ] CHK005 MySQL persistence covers workflow sessions, artifacts, and review records
- [ ] CHK006 The primary user entry is a GUI or web interface

## Model & Quality Compliance

- [ ] CHK007 Every agent uses a publicly available Hugging Face model with role-fit rationale
- [ ] CHK008 Integration, review-gate, and end-to-end validation are defined where required
- [ ] CHK009 Important artifacts exist in Chinese, Japanese, and English where required

## Style Compliance

- [ ] CHK010 Naming conventions are consistent across backend, database, API, and frontend code
- [ ] CHK011 Code paths remain simple and unnecessary abstractions are avoided
- [ ] CHK012 Review findings and follow-up actions are tracked to closure

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
